title: Personal/Grooming Supplies * Homeless Youth - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Personal/Grooming Supplies * Homeless Youth 10 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Homeless Families Homeless Youth Homelessness Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ARK PROJECT OF COOS COUNTY ARK OF COOS COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides basic needs, essential support services, crisis intervention, and advocacy. Services include a drop-in center, food pantry, children's clothing, school supplies, personal grooming supplies, feminine hygiene supplies, mentoring, information and referrals for other assistance agencies, and assistance in applying for identification. 817 S 10th Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Ages 0-21 and their families who are experiencing homelessness Hours: School year: Monday-Thursday 1pm-5pm Summer: Tuesday-Wednesday 1pm-5pm (541) 267-3104 7115 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARTISTS MENTORSHIP PROGRAM AMP DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to music lessons, art workshops, recording studio sessions, roleplaying game sessions, cultural outings, meals, hygiene supplies, clothing, first aid supplies, and mentorship services. 16 NW Broadway Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Homeless youth ages 15-25 Hours: Monday-Thursday 4pm-8pm, Friday by appointment only Email (503) 481-4970 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTS WITH A MISSION HEARTS WITH A MISSION MEDFORD YOUTH SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Youth shelter providing temporary emergency shelter for up to 120 days with written parent/guardian consent or 72 hours without written consent. Services for sheltered and unsheltered youth include case management, mentoring, housing search assistance, and counseling services. Bathing facilities, laundry facilities, a clothing closet, and personal grooming supplies are also provided. School supplies are for enrolled participants only. 517 Edwards Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Homeless or at-risk youth ages 10-17 Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 646-7385 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOMEPLATE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly drop-in center where youth can access food, clothing, showers, and hygiene products and receive referrals for other resources. Crafts, sports, games, and other activities may also be held at the drop-in centers. Outreach workers are also available to meet youth anywhere in Washington County to discuss resources. Operates education and employment programs with one-on-one support for job search, application assistance, skills training, resume and cover letter creation, interview coaching, school reconnection, navigating financial aid, enrolling in college or trade schools, and life skills. 12685 SW 4th Street Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Youth ages 12-24 who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness. Youth may access services until their 25th birthday. Hours: Monday/Wednesday 3pm-5pm and 6pm-8pm, Tuesday 2pm-5pm Email (503) 608-7252 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOMEPLATE HOMEPLATE THURSDAY NIGHT DROP IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly drop-in center where youth can access food, clothing, showers, and hygiene products and receive referrals for other resources. Crafts, sports, games, and other activities may also be held at the drop-in centers. Outreach workers are also available to meet youth anywhere in Washington County to discuss resources. Operates education and employment programs with one-on-one support for job search, application assistance, skills training, resume and cover letter creation, interview coaching, school reconnection, navigating financial aid, enrolling in college or trade schools, and life skills. 494 E Main Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Youth ages 12-24 who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness. Youth may access services until their 25th birthday. Hours: Thursday 6pm-8pm Email (503) 608-7252 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 223 SE M Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 841-1974 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 500 Monroe Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 608-6868 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH DOWNTOWN DROP-IN DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community meals six days per week. Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Provides information about community resources such as education, job training, employment, housing, and LGBTQIA2S+ supports. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. 820 SW Oak Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-10am, 1pm-3pm, and 4pm-6:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm and 4pm-6:30pm. Meals served for the first hour of each shift. Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH CONNECT DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community meals six days per week. Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Provides information about community resources such as education, job training, employment, housing, and LGBTQIA2S+ supports. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. 43 SE 102 Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Ages 12-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-10am, 1pm-3pm, and 4pm-6:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm and 4pm-6:30pm. Meals served for the first hour of each shift. Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH OPPORTUNITY CENTER DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. Also connects youth to other services like education, job training, employment, housing, LGBTQIA2S+ supports, and more. 470 SE 165th Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-6pm Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARK PROJECT OF COOS COUNTY ARK OF COOS COUNTY ARTISTS MENTORSHIP PROGRAM AMP DROP-IN CENTER HEARTS WITH A MISSION YOUTH SHELTER HOMEPLATE HOMEPLATE HOMEPLATE HOMEPLATE THURSDAY NIGHT DROP IN CENTER MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH DROP-IN DAY SERVICES NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH DROP-IN DAY SERVICES NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH DROP-IN DAY SERVICES
